characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Prime, the Autobot commander of the forces on Earth, leads the team through the battles against Unicron (twice) and saves Cybertron and reality from Unicron.

characters.name: Optimus Prime
characters.description: Prime, the Autobot commander of the forces on Earth, leads the team through the battles against Unicron (twice) and saves Cybertron and reality from Unicron.

characters.name: Hot Shot
characters.description: The resident team rookie, Hot Shot, has learned many hard lessons throughout his career, much to the chagrin of some of the older bots.

characters.name: Red Alert
characters.description: TheCombat Medicof the Autobots may seem like theNon-Action Guy, but harm his friends, and you will regret it.

characters.name: Smokescreen / Hoist
characters.description: Smokescreen originally intended to be one of the team members who accompanied Optimus Prime to Earth. He entered the space bridge portal late, so he materialized sometime later than they did. He is later involved in a serious battle that leads to a significantMid-Season Upgrade. Big Fun: A huge Autobot with a fun personality. Boisterous Bruiser: He's a huge, chunky Autobot with a penchant for brute strength and being energetic and fun. Commuting on a Bus: in the Dreamwave comics, he'd go long stretches without appearances. Demonization: The kids assumedthat he was a Decepticon when he first appeared. Of course, the fact that he chased them all over the place and didn't introduce himself properly probably didn't help much.Micron Legendsubverts this as the kids don't initially mention being with the Autobots, so Smokescreen might have thought they were with the Decepticons, and he later apologizes for attacking them by mistake.

characters.name: Scavenger
characters.description: Scavenger is an old war veteran,who just so happens to be the Autobot who was Optimus Prime's brother-in-arms. Adaptational Heroism:Scavenger was a genuine Decepticon inGeneration One,whereas he's amolefor the Autobots.The Dreamwave and Panini comics entirely excise his time pretending to be a Decepticon. Scavenger was a genuine Decepticon inGeneration One,whereas he's amolefor the Autobots. The Dreamwave and Panini comics entirely excise his time pretending to be a Decepticon. Badass Cape: Transformers as a whole don't need capes. His is purely forRule of Cool.

characters.name: Blurr
characters.description: Cool and aloof, Blurr is one of the later additions to the Autobot ranks. He initially thought of the Mini-Cons as mere tools, a thought which Hot Shot slowly shot down. Adaptation Personality Change: In G1, Blurr was a motormouthed and talkative Autobot, whereas in here, he's more stoic and aloof who is not used to communicating. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Disappears afterArmadaand never reappears again (unless you think the toyline character fromCybertronis him). Cold Sniper: Initially very aloof and one who attacks from far and away. Dub Name Change: He was initially named Silverbolt inMicron Legendand was renamed Blurr in the English dub ofArmadaafter a G1 blue car.

characters.name: Jetfire
characters.description: He's the second-in-command of the Autobots on Cybertron. Jetfire left Cybertron and arrived on Earth to help Optimus and the others.

characters.name: Sideswipe
characters.description: Hot Shot's newest partner. It didn't take long for Sideswipe to be captured by Hot Shot's friend-turned-foe, Wheeljack. Sideswipe was saved, but the experience taught him a harsh lesson about war, leading him to sober up quickly. Distressed Dude: In "Past II," after being abducted and chained up in a burning industrial complex. Dub Name Change: His Japanese name inMicron Legendis Stepper, shared with a Targetmaster fromHeadmasters.noteG1 Stepper is known in the western toyline as "Ricochet" Hero-Worshipper: He joins the team on Earth in the first place due to being a Blurr fanboy since the latter saved him way back on Cybertron. In Name Only: He's not a dirty-handed and somewhat agressive fighter that G1 Sideswipe was described as. In fact,Wheeljackis more of an adaptation of G1 Sideswipe in combat-style and looks. He also lacks anything resembling G1 Ricochet.

characters.name: Overload
characters.description: The Big Guy: Dreamwave Overload serves as the largest of Jetfire's Cybertron contingent. Dark and Troubled Past: He became a Powerlinx junkie, which nearly killed him. Depending on the Writer: The nature of his relationship with Rollout varies. The Dreamwave comic doesn't give any indication whether Rollout is a separate individual, while the toy pack-in comics have them acting individually. Anime Overload doesn't say a word. Flat Character: Anime Overload is a set of guns for Optimus. Nothing more. Fusion Dance: He and Rollout were medically merged in order to save Overload's life.

characters.name: Rhinox
characters.description: The Cameo: Makes a one-off appearance in the anime. Dropped a Bridge on Him:Ask Vector Primeconfirms the show Rhinox was killed in the fight with Unicron. Number Two: In the Dreamwave comics, he's Jetfire's right-hand guy. Reforged into a Minion: Dreamwave Rhinox was abducted by Unicron alongside Cheetor, Airazor, and Terrorsaur and turned into one of his heralds.

characters.name: Sentinel Minor
characters.description: Hot Shot's former platoon commander, whose attitude during a particular battle had far more reaching consequences than he realized. Adaptation Deviation: This version of Sentinel is subordinate to Optimus Prime rather than his immediate predecessor. Adaptation Name Change: Most versions of Sentinel are called "Sentinel Prime", but this one is called "Sentinel Minor". Drill Sergeant Nasty: LikeAnimatedSentinel, he doesn't treat his subordinates with much respect when they disobey a direct order. Jerkass: He refuses to save Wheeljack because he felt it wasn't worth the waste of resources. When Hot Shot tried to go back and rescue his friend, Sentinel decked him for it; however, he's not nearly as bad asAnimatedSentinel.

characters.name: Megatron
characters.description: Leader of the Decepticons. Megatron is a malicious, all-powerful leader. He is a refined and focused general who treats his men with little care yet still infuses them with genuine respect for him and the Decepticon cause.

characters.name: Starscream
characters.description: Megatron's poorly-treated second in command. Starscream desires to prove himself worthy of his leader's respect, which turns to loathing the more mistreatment and contempt that Megatron piles onto him. Over the series, Starscream reassesses his loyalty—to Megatron and the Decepticons as a whole.

characters.name: Demolishor
characters.description: Demolishor is extremely strong and acts very seriously at all times. He is wholly devoted to Megatron and the Decepticon cause, unquestionably following orders at every turn.

characters.name: Cyclonus/Snowcat
characters.description: Cyclonus has a very "trigger-happy" personality, laughing and cackling as he fires his weapons almost entirely at random on the battlefield.

characters.name: Thrust
characters.description: Thrust is Megatron's top intelligence officer and master tactician. Adaptational Badass: The original Thrust was little more than a generic goon. This Thrust is a brilliant strategist, noted for several key victories against the Autobots. Adaptational Heroism: In the comics, Thrust is not as cowardly or treacherous,and by the time ofEnergonhe's one of the few Cons still loyal to Megatron. Adaptational Intelligence:G1Thrust was a fairly low-ranking Decepticon and just a simple-minded thug at the end of the day, whereasArmadaThrust is a renowned military tactician.However, he's not as smart as he thinks he is. Adaptational Villainy: While Thrust is a Decepticon,this series kicks up his villainy with him siding withUnicronover Megatron. He is even worse in the English dub, where he does not care if Unicron destroys Cybertron.

characters.name: Tidal Wave/Mirage
characters.description: The largest of the Decepticons, the lumbering giant Tidal Wave, proved a mighty foe for the Autobots.

characters.name: Wheeljack
characters.description: Wheeljack was formerly an Autobot serving under Hot Shot. When Wheeljack was pinned beneath some debris during a fire, Hot Shot was forced to leave him to get help, but his superiors prevented him from returning. Wheeljack believed that Hot Shot had abandoned him, and when Megatron found and rescued him, he swore loyalty to the Decepticon commander. Abandonment-Induced Animosity: He believes that Hot Shot abandoned him to die earlier in the war. The reality is somewhat different.The original JapaneseMicron Legendplays this straight, as Hot Shot did leave Wheeljack to die out of cowardice and fear, and Hot Shot telling him he was going to get help was just an excuse; making Wheeljack's grudge towards Hot Shot more justified inMicron Legend. Adaptation Personality Change:His personality is very different betweenArmadaandMicron Legends. InArmadaWheeljack is someone consumed by hatred and a drive for revenge, willingly being a Decepticon after Megatron saved him in the past and refusing to forgive Hot Shot forabandoninghim,until the threat of Unicron proves too great and he defends Hot Shot when Galvatron tries to strike him down, the two becoming friends again. InMicron LegendsRampage neverstoppedbeing Hot Shot's friend, as while the beliefand truth thatHot Shot abandoned himhurt, he doesn'twantrevenge on him, but heowes his lifeandloyaltyto Megatron, so he'd rather Hot Shot just see him as another enemy on the battlefield.The ending to his debut two-parter is where this is most apparent. InArmada's "Past II", Wheeljack simply missed his shot at Hot Shot despite it being aimed directly at his chest, with Wheeljack reiterating he will never forgive Hot Shot. InMicron Legends"Past, Part 2: Conclusion", Optimus and Silverbolt (Blurr) note that Rampage missed his shoton purpose, realizing that Rampage was trying to get Hot Shot to forget about him andonly see him as an enemyso Hot Shotcould move on from his guilt, with it being implied Rampage actually forgave Hot Shotlong

characters.name: Rad White
characters.description: Rad is the one who reactivated the Mini-Con ship while exploring some caves and is inadvertently responsible for the Cybertronian War restarting. All There in the Manual: WhenArmadastarts, he and Carlos are 12 years old. He is also aNature Loverand enjoys outdoor sports. Audience Surrogate: His reactions are similar to ours throughout the war between the Autobots and the Decepticons. Battle in the Center of the Mind: He once helpedthe Mini-Cons break Unicron's' mental hold on them. Beware the Nice Ones: May only be a human, but he's got guts.

characters.name: Carlos Lopez
characters.description: Carlos is Rad's friend and was with him when he reactivated the Mini-Cons. All There in the Manual: He and Rad are both 12 at the start ofArmada, and they've known each other since kindergarten. He loves sports, hip-hop, and rap. He is also not a great student but an excellent mechanic, something the cartoon omits but is featured in the comics. Audience Surrogate: His character and reactions are very relatable to us when it comes to seeing things between the Autobots and the Decepticons. Ordinary High-School Student: Also attended school when he got caught up in the war. Plucky Comic Relief: He's full of jokes... primarily bad ones.

characters.name: Alexis Thi Dang
characters.description: Alexis, along with Rad and Carlos, befriended the Street Action Team Mini-Cons and Autobots on Earth. She frequently helped them by teaching them the ways of Earth. All There in the Manual: Her surname, Thi Dang. She's also 11 years old whenArmadabegins. Asian and Nerdy: Alexis is the brains of the trio and excels in academics. As stated in the liner notes of the second series soundtrack and on the now-discontinued TV Tokyo website forLegends of the Microns, she's also the daughter of Vietnamese immigrants. Audience Surrogate: Provides some reactions which we can relate to on some of the show's biggest moments. Child Prodigy: She always gets excellent grades, which probably explains why Alexis is in the 8th grade at age 11. Plus, she's a natural computer whiz as she interfaces with the Autobot base's system through her laptop.

characters.name: Billy
characters.description: Billy and Fred start as bully classmates of Rad, Alexis, and Carlos but become their friends after the trio and the Autobots save them from the Decepticons. All There in the Manual: The producers planned to write him and Fred out of the show at some point but never got around to it. Hence, their staying through to the finale was accidental. Bully Turned Buddy: He and Fred were bullies towards Rad, Alexis, and Carlos, but they later became friends after joing the Autobot and Decepticon war. Useless Protagonist: Compared to the main three children, and even compared to Fred, Billy brings absolutely nothing to the table as far as pushing the narrative goes. He has no particular skills, personality or really much of anything, he's simply there just to be seen from start to finish.

characters.name: Fred
characters.description: Fred is a classmate of Rad, Alexis, and Carlos, who bullies them alongside Billy as his right-hand guy as Billy has the dominant personality. Fred eventually becomes friends with them and joins the Autobot team. All There in the Manual: The producers planned to write him and Billy out of the show after some time but never got around to it. Hence, their staying through to the finale was accidental. Bully Turned Buddy: He and Billy were bullies towards Rad, Alexis, and Carlos, but they later became friends after joing the Autobot and Decepticon war. Cowardly Lion: He's not much of a fighter, but he does have moments of this when Megatron captures Rad as a hostage after receiving the Autobots' Mini-Cons as ransom. Fred uses Laserbeak to taze the tyrant for breaking his promise and successfully buy the Autobots a brief chance of saving Rad. Dub Name Change: He was named Jim inMicron Legendand renamed Fred in the English dub ofArmada.

characters.name: Unicron
characters.description: A primal force of evil, his origins lost to the mists of time. He is the embodiment of all darkness and hate that lurks in the hearts of all beings.
